title: Peer-trigger attempts and peer execution are separate claims
summary: The shared coordination model requires teams to leave evidence plus a peer trigger request, while the trigger ledger explicitly describes requests as durable requests for a later peer run. Therefore a successful trigger call, schedule update, or appended request proves only that coordination was attempted; it does not prove the peer executed, produced artifacts, or completed its queue item.

observed: CURRENT_STATE.json requires evidence plus a peer trigger request.
TRIGGER_REQUESTS.json states that requests may wait for the next scheduled peer run.
The inspected trigger request array was empty at its current blob.
inferred: Trigger evidence and execution evidence require separate status fields to prevent false completion claims.
proposed: Future team evidence packets should record trigger_attempt_status, trigger_mechanism, peer_execution_observed, and peer_output_locator separately.
superseded: Any reporting convention that treats a successful schedule update or trigger request as proof that the peer ran.
unresolved: No repository-level receipt schema currently binds a trigger attempt to a later peer output without conflating the two.

falsification_or_review_route: Reject or revise this record if the shared operating files are changed to define trigger acknowledgement as execution proof, or if a durable receipt mechanism cryptographically or transactionally binds each trigger to a verified peer run and output. Until then, require independent peer-run evidence.
